title: Imbricaria tenella
taxonomy: Imbricaria tenella is a species of sea snail, and it belongs to the family Liotiidae. This family is part of the larger group known as the Patellogastropoda, which is a superfamily of small to medium-sized sea snails. The Liotiidae family is primarily found in marine environments around the world, with a focus on tropical and subtropical regions. Imbricaria tenella is a specific example of this family and is categorized under the kingdom Animalia and the phylum Mollusca. Further classification may include its classification within the class Gastropoda.

intro: Imbricaria tenella, also known as Tenella, is a plant species native to tropical forests, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in treating various health conditions including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Imbricaria tenella include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made from Imbricaria tenella include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
